text.title: Blue Ocean Four Actions Framework
text.summary: Four Actions framework for creating uncontested market space, with survivorship-bias warning and example constraints.

rule.statement: Run Four Actions only after a competitor-analysis document exists. Without a baseline of what competitors currently offer, "eliminate" has no meaning.
rule.rationale: Blue Ocean analysis without competitive data produces aspirational fiction. The Strategy Canvas requires actual competitor offerings plotted on the Y-axis before the new curve can be drawn.

list.item: Eliminate: which factors the industry takes for granted should be removed entirely? (typically costly or assumed-required features users rarely value)
Reduce: which factors should be reduced well below the industry standard? (over-engineered for actual user needs)
Raise: which factors should be raised above the industry standard? (currently underserved relative to what buyers actually want)
Create: which factors should be created that the industry has never offered? (generates new demand or new buyer population)

section.title: Strategy Canvas
section.p: Plot the X-axis as key competing factors (price, features, support, onboarding, integrations, etc.) and the Y-axis as level offered (low to high). Plot 3+ real competitors from the competitor-analysis JSON before drawing the new value curve. Require 3 real data points minimum; curves drawn without data are marketing.

rule.statement: Force the Strategy Canvas to plot against at least 3 real competitor data points pulled from competitive-analysis output. LLM-drawn curves default to showing the "winning case" without real competition plotted.
rule.rationale: Survivorship bias: agents produce a strategy canvas that looks like the case study (Cirque, Yellow Tail) without actually plotting competitor offerings. The resulting curve is aspirational, not analytical.

antipattern.description: Citing Cirque du Soleil, Yellow Tail wine, Nintendo Wii, or iTunes as Blue Ocean examples
antipattern.reason: These are the 5-7 textbook cases cited because they succeeded. The failure rate of "create new factor" bets is high and invisible. Require post-2018 examples and reject the canonical textbook cases.

antipattern.description: Running Blue Ocean analysis on a single niche without a comparison set
antipattern.reason: Blue Ocean is only meaningful when contrasted against a current red ocean; without it you cannot identify what to eliminate or reduce.

text.reference: Blue Ocean Four Actions canvas template
